title: buildkit: add `from=` field to `bind` and `cache` mounts so images can be used as source
body:
Following commit adds buildkit like support for `from` field to `--mount=type=bind`
and `--mount=type=cache` so images and stage can be used as mount source.

Usage looks like
```dockerfile
RUN --mount=type=bind,source=.,from=<your-image>,target=/path ls /path
```
and
```dockerfile
RUN --mount=type=cache,from=<your-image>,target=/path ls /path
```
Closes: https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues/3501

@nalind @TomSweeneyRedHat Made requested corrections. Could you please take a look again.

LGTM
@nalind does it LGTY?

The tests need to also exercise `source` values other than ".", including paths that start with "/", "./", and other values (all of which they need to verify are interpreted as being relative to root of the image or stage specified for the "from").  They also need to check that `from` values that reference other stages in multi-stage builds work as expected.  These options should also be tested with `buildah run`, where "from" should probably be able to point to other containers, to line up with how the `--from` flag is treated for `buildah copy`.
@nalind Thanks i'll get these tests in.

> > The tests need to also exercise `source` values other than ".", including paths that start with "/", "./", and other values (all of which they need to verify are interpreted as being relative to root of the image or stage specified for the "from"). They also need to check that `from` values that reference other stages in multi-stage builds work as expected. These options should also be tested with `buildah run`, where "from" should probably be able to point to other containers, to line up with how the `--from` flag is treated for `buildah copy`.

Its needed to mount different stages.

ping @rhatdan @nalind

Update: Splitting `se-linux` change into a different PR and will take label from the first container and let c/storage handle se-linux. [Affected feature]: **mutistage-from-mounting with selinux enabled**.

PS: I'll also rebase this PR

> Update: Splitting `se-linux` change into a different PR and will take label from the first container and let c/storage handle se-linux. [Affected feature]: **mutistage-from-mounting with selinux enabled**.

Rebased after this. @nalind Could you PTAL
@flouthoc looks like you need a rebase.  The changes LGTM, but honestly I just did a skim review and would like at least @nalind to review.

This needs more tests.  For example, the `Dockerfile` test for cache mounts doesn't exercise the `source` or `id` flags, or cases where `from` points to an earlier stage in the build, and the test for bind mounts doesn't test what happens when `from` isn't specified.

> This needs more tests. For example, the `Dockerfile` test for cache mounts doesn't exercise the `source` or `id` flags, or cases where `from` points to an earlier stage in the build, and the test for bind mounts doesn't test what happens when `from` isn't specified.

@nalind Thanks. Added new tests for

* Cache
   * using `from` previous stages. In a multistage build.
   * exercising `source` with relative paths across stages. In a multistage build.
   * exercising `id` across stages. However practically `id` has no benefits  when using with `from`.
* Bind
   * pointing to invalid ( i.e `empty` ) `from`.
   * `from` specified without `source`. 
   
and some of tests are overlapping with https://github.com/containers/buildah/pull/3548/files and https://github.com/containers/buildah/pull/3574

Please let me know if any additional tests are needed.
@nalind friendly ping. :)
@flouthoc What is happening with this PR?
@rhatdan I somehow didn't get github notification on this. I'll fix @nalind  remarks.
@nalind Thanks I'll add requested tests for `buildah run` and `buildah build` and resolve nits.

@nalind Added requested tests and resolved all the nits. One of them is unresolved but i have added comment for reason behind it. https://github.com/containers/buildah/pull/3590#discussion_r745155252

New tests added
* ``buildah run`: only simple `from` test is added for `run` since multistage is not supported and other scenarios are covered in `bud.tests`
* `buildah build`: tests for multiple `--mount` stmts to make sure code is not getting confused.

Hi @nalind ,

Resolved all your feedback points and added requested test. Except for last test. PTAL

> Needs tests for mounts with `type=bind` with no `from`, which should use the build context as the default root location, potentially in combination with `source` pointing to a relative location inside the build context.

Following request tests without `from` were already added here: https://github.com/containers/buildah/pull/3548

>Needs tests where multistage builds use `type=bind` with `from` pointing to a previous stage's nickname.

By nickname i am assuming `FROM base as <nickname>` added such case in this PR -> [Test](https://github.com/containers/buildah/blob/08e421040b4763f43ef0a7fcadd9c01c1aaee96f/tests/bud/buildkit-mount-from/Dockerfilemultistagefrom)

 >Test cases that expect mounts of `type=cache` to recognize `from` values as image names shouldn't be expected to succeed, as `from` in that context can only refer to other build stages, and not images. 

Added this case where test is expected to fail -> [Test](https://github.com/containers/buildah/blob/08e421040b4763f43ef0a7fcadd9c01c1aaee96f/tests/bud/buildkit-mount-from/Dockerfilecachefromimage)
 
 >`buildah run` test cases should exercise cases where `type=bind,from=...` and `type=cache,from=...` specify container names as `from` values. Also, please refrain from marking a comment as resolved when we haven't reached agreement.

I was unable to add this as following PR is only scoped to `buildkit` so as of now it can only mount `images` and `stages`. Mounting container seems like a `buildah` specific feature so I'll do that in a new PR if that works.

@nalind Resolved all the comments. PTAL

For this https://github.com/containers/buildah/pull/3590#discussion_r779789753 also made sure that we only return `imagename` when image was actually mounted not for `stages` or `containers`. So now we only attempt `cleanup` on actual mounted images.
I'm not entirely sure we've got the behavior right for writable bind mounts, otherwise LGTM.
@flouthoc needs rebase.

> I'm not entirely sure we've got the behavior right for writable bind mounts, otherwise LGTM.

@nalind  This is implemented in latest commit also added a test to verify this. https://github.com/containers/buildah/blob/fb163aa67b71f1251d64e32af8ee2821f516194c/tests/bud.bats#L3743
Sadly @flouthoc needs a rebase.
/lgtm
